page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m8-vws.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 10 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 46.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.6 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU, which provides 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. It does not include local storage and relies on network-attached storage, operating with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth remains average across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks. For specialized tasks, the instance achieves average performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing, but struggles with single-threaded compression and decompression.

Given the fractional GPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring hardware acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. It is suitable for entry-level LLM inference on small models, where it achieves average text generation speeds, though performance drops significantly on larger 7B models. The hardware profile is best suited for lightweight GPU-accelerated workloads, virtual workstations, basic image processing, and general-purpose applications that benefit from moderate multi-core CPU capacity and dedicated VRAM.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m8-vws.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 10 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 46.0 GB of RAM. It includes a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM, but lacks local storage, relying on network-attached storage with a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmarks show weak single-core CPU performance, but multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth reach average levels. The fractional GPU makes this instance a cost-effective option for workloads that require hardware acceleration without the cost of a full GPU. It is best suited for virtual workstations, moderate multi-core processing, image rendering, and lightweight machine learning inference on smaller models.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m8-vws.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 10 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 46 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU with 8 GB VRAM, designed for virtual workstations and light AI inference.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m8-vws.2xlarge features 10 vCPUs, 46 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU for cost-effective accelerated workloads.
tagline: A cost-effective, GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and fractional graphics memory for entry-level parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: 10 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz
Fractional NVIDIA T4 GPU providing 8 GB of dedicated VRAM
46.0 GB of system memory with 4.6 GB per core allocation
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for virtual workstations, image processing, and light LLM inference
